paper_title: Efficacy of indoor air purification in treating Artemisia (mugwort) pollen allergic rhinitis: study protocol for a randomised controlled trial

methods.description: This is a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial that tests active and inactive versions of an air purifier. The trial aims to evaluate the efficacy of air purifiers in treating allergic rhinitis caused by Artemisia pollen.

steps.description: AR patients sensitive to the allergens produced by Artemisia pollen will be assigned randomly to two groups of equal numbers.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly assign patients to two groups for clinical trial

steps.description: All patients will accept a 4-week treatment period and a 4-week observation period.
steps.searchable_summary: define treatment and observation periods for patients

steps.description: Patient evaluation will be undertaken at baseline (day 0) and on days 7, 14, 21, 28, and 56.
steps.searchable_summary: schedule patient evaluations at specified intervals

methods.description: Study activities will be conducted at different settings, including patient enrollment, specimen and data collection, biochemical examinations, and data analyses.

steps.description: Patient enrollment and specimen and data collection will be conducted in the Outpatient Department of the First Hospital of Yulin (Guangxi, China).
steps.searchable_summary: enroll patients and collect specimens and data at hospital

steps.description: Biochemical examinations will be undertaken by King Med Diagnostics (Guangzhou, China).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct biochemical examinations at diagnostic center

steps.description: Data analyses will be performed at the University of Toronto (Toronto, ON, Canada).
